qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Denial of Service through uncontrolled brace expansion memory consumption
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same package and broad denial-of-service class, but this finding identifies braceExpand()/brace-expansion materialization and globSet memory growth rather than the advisory's repeated-* wildcard regex backtracking with a missing literal.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause: minimatch repeated '*' wildcards compiling into multiple [^/]*? regex groups followed by a non-matching literal, causing exponential regex backtracking.
evidence: The advisory GHSA-3ppc-4f35-3m26/CVE-2026-26996 describes ReDoS in minimatch when many consecutive '*' wildcards precede a literal absent from the test string.
minimatch_brace_expansion_dos reports brace expansion through braceExpand()/brace-expansion and globSet array allocation, which is a different vulnerable mechanism from wildcard regex backtracking.
